hits.content: Set default FILE_UPLOAD_PERMISSIONS to 0o644 in Django to ensure consistent file permissions regardless of whether MemoryUploadedFile or TemporaryUploadedFile is used for temporary storage
The URLValidator fix should also update test cases in tests/validators/invalid_urls.txt to ensure invalid URLs with unencoded username/password characters are rejected
Set default FILE_UPLOAD_PERMISSIONS to 0o644 in Django to ensure consistent file permissions regardless of whether MemoryUploadedFile or TemporaryUploadedFile is used for temporary storage
The user is working on fixing a Django issue where HttpResponse does not handle memoryview objects correctly (issue #11133 in django/django repository).
The URLValidator fix should also update test cases in tests/validators/invalid_urls.txt to ensure invalid URLs with unencoded username/password characters are rejected
URLValidator must reject URLs with unencoded ':', '@', or '/' in username/password fields per RFC 1738 section 3.1; example invalid URL: http://foo/bar@example.com
The user is fixing Django issue django/django #11451: ModelBackend.authenticate() should return None early when username or password is None to avoid an unnecessary database query.
